items.title: Lawfulness, Fairness, Transparency
items.description: Personal data must be processed lawfully, fairly, and transparently.

items.title: Purpose Limitation
items.description: Data must be collected for specified purposes and not used incompatibly.

items.title: Data Minimization
items.description: Only data necessary for the purpose should be processed.

items.title: Integrity and Confidentiality
items.description: Data must be protected against unauthorized access, disclosure, or loss.

items.title: Lawfulness of Processing
items.description: Processing is lawful only under valid legal bases such as consent or contract.

items.title: Right of Access
items.description: Data subjects can access their personal data and processing details.

items.title: Right to Rectification
items.description: Data subjects can correct inaccurate personal data without undue delay.

items.title: Right to Erasure
items.description: Data subjects can request deletion of personal data where legally applicable.

items.title: Data Protection by Design and by Default
items.description: Systems must embed privacy safeguards by design and default settings.

items.title: Records of Processing Activities
items.description: Controllers and processors must maintain records of processing activities.

items.title: Security of Processing
items.description: Implement appropriate technical and organizational security measures.

items.title: Breach Notification
items.description: Personal data breaches must be detected, handled, and notified on time.
